video.title: ProLog Promo Video
video.caption: A walkthrough of the ProLog apprenticeship companion app

title: ProLog
tagline: A journey companion for neurodivergent apprentices
subtitle: A journey companion for neurodivergent apprentices
summary: A centralized apprenticeship companion helping neurodivergent apprentices track progress, reduce cognitive overload, and build confidence through clear visualization and structured tools.

overview.title: Overview
overview.description:
Apprenticeship requires over 6,000 hours. That's years of work, spread across job sites, classrooms, and bureaucratic systems—often without a clear sense of whether progress is on track.

Early research consistently revealed the same finding: confusion was pushing people out. Not lack of ability. Not lack of motivation. Just confusion about current standing and next steps.

The challenge became designing something that answered one question clearly: Where am I right now?

problem.title: The Problem
problem.description:
Apprentices in British Columbia navigate a surprisingly fragmented system. Hours are logged in one place, competencies tracked in another, and certification requirements buried in dense PDFs.

For neurotypical users, this is frustrating. For neurodivergent apprentices—who may already struggle with executive function, time blindness, or information overload—it becomes a genuine barrier to completion.

The system wasn't designed to be hostile. It just wasn't designed with these users in mind.

research.title: Research & Insights
research.description:
Policy and documentation review (SkilledTradesBC), stakeholder conversations with industry partners, persona development based on lived experience, and team reflection sessions (informed by neurodivergent team members) formed the research foundation.

Key findings revealed: Progress tracking is scattered across multiple platforms with no single source of truth. Official systems are desktop-first and difficult to use on job sites. Discrepancies between employer-logged and self-logged hours create anxiety. Neurodivergent users experience amplified cognitive load from inconsistent interfaces.

Due to limited participant access, the research leaned on available strengths: lived experience from team members, publicly available documentation, and stakeholder conversations.

images.alt: Research findings and user pain points

challenges.title: Early Challenges
challenges.description:
Initial project scope proved overly broad. The ambition to address financial stress, employer communication, exam preparation, and progress tracking simultaneously created design paralysis.

Narrowing the focus to neurodivergent apprentices specifically proved transformative. This constraint allowed deeper empathy-driven design rather than generic solutions.

pivots.reason: A narrower scope enabled deeper, empathy-driven design rather than generic assumptions

lofi.title: Lo-Fi Exploration
lofi.description:
Early sketches prioritized understanding information hierarchy and scanning speed. The question driving the exploration was: if an apprentice opens this app on a break, what should they see in the first three seconds?

Progress bars, hour counts, and next-step prompts emerged as core elements. All other information served as secondary context.

iterations.title: High-Fidelity Designs
iterations.description:
High-fidelity designs emphasized a calming aesthetic. Soft colors, generous spacing, and typography designed to avoid clinical or overwhelming presentation formed the visual foundation.

The dashboard functioned as the emotional center of the application. Progress information—hours completed, hours remaining, competencies achieved—was made immediately visible rather than hidden behind navigation layers.
improvements: Prioritized calm through soft colors and generous spacing
Made dashboard the emotional center with immediate progress visibility
Used clear typography to avoid clinical or overwhelming feel
Designed for offline-first architecture for job site use
Created discrepancy detection for hour tracking

solution.title: Final Solution
solution.description: ProLog provides four core features: a Progress Dashboard that acts as a GPS for the apprenticeship journey, an Hours Discrepancy Detector that compares user-logged hours against official records, a Financial Resources Hub with links to grants and funding programs, and AI Practice Quizzes that generate unique practice questions based on trade competencies.

features.title: Progress Dashboard
features.description: A centralized view of hours completed, hours remaining, competencies achieved, and certification progress. No digging required—the answer to 'where am I?' is always visible.

features.title: Hours Discrepancy Detector
features.description: Compares user-logged hours against official records and flags mismatches. Users can document discrepancies and generate reports for their employers.

features.title: Financial Resources Hub
features.description: Links to grants, funding programs, and itemized cost breakdowns for apprenticeship expenses.

features.title: AI Practice Quizzes
features.description: Generates unique practice questions based on trade competencies. Designed to build confidence before official exams.

validation.title: Validation & Testing
validation.description: The prototype was tested with a small group, yielding unexpected feedback: relief. Users described the dashboard as a 'GPS for my apprenticeship.' They appreciated that they didn't have to hunt for information—it was just there. The hours tracker resonated especially strongly. One participant noted they'd never realized they could document discrepancies themselves.

quotes.text: It's like a GPS for my apprenticeship.
quotes.author: Test participant

quotes.text: I never realized I could document discrepancies myself.
quotes.author: Test participant

insights.title: Clarity functions as an emotional design outcome
insights.description: User reports of feeling 'relieved' correlated with specific design decisions about information hierarchy and visual presentation, not merely information architecture.

insights.title: Scope constraint improves design quality
insights.description: Restricting feature scope to a single user population enabled deeper, more empathetic design work.

insights.title: Progress visualization drives motivation
insights.description: Visualizing distance already traveled proved equally important as showing what steps remain.

insights.title: Qualitative research provides valid insights
insights.description: Lived experience and small-sample qualitative feedback generated insights of equal validity to large-scale quantitative studies.

nextSteps.title: Next Steps
items: Pursue data partnerships with SkilledTradesBC to enable real-time syncing
Expand accessibility features including screen reader optimization and high-contrast modes
Develop offline-friendly experiences for use on job sites with limited connectivity

outcomes.title: Impact & Reflection
outcomes.description: Apprentices can quickly understand progress and next steps. Modular design allows future expansion to additional trades and certifications. Collaboration strengthened by research and iterative design process. Editorial layout and visual progress tracking increase engagement and motivation. Lessons learned: Visual progress indicators are essential for user motivation. Clear language reduces confusion. Whitespace and editorial design improve content hierarchy and readability.

promotionalMaterials.title: Promotional Materials
promotionalMaterials.description: ProLog's promotional ecosystem spans digital and physical channels, creating multiple touchpoints for both apprentices and institutional stakeholders.

materials.title: Website & Brand Center
materials.description: The Framer website serves as the primary brand anchor, establishing ProLog's visual identity and core messaging: 'What if your apprenticeship had a clear roadmap?' The design communicates the value proposition through six benefit statements—Visual Pathway, Hour Tracking, Competencies, School Details, Financial Support, and Reminders—each articulating a specific pain point the product addresses. The team bios and partnership section signal credibility and organizational backing, positioning ProLog as a thoughtful, human-centered solution rather than generic software.

materials.title: Logo & Visual System
materials.description: The ProLog logo functions as the visual anchor across all collateral. Consistent application across the website, stickers, business cards, and social content creates brand recognition. The metallic abstract shapes on the landing page convey forward motion and clarity—visual metaphors for the 'roadmap' messaging.

materials.title: Business Cards & Brochures
materials.description: Printed collateral serves dual purposes: distribution to apprentices through training organizations and as conversation starters at industry partnerships. The business card format is optimized for job site environments (durable, pocket-sized), while tri-fold brochures provide deeper messaging for coordinators and employers, explaining features and partnership benefits in a format that survives desk drawers and email forwarding.

materials.title: Instagram & Social Content
materials.description: Short-form social posts target apprentices directly with benefit-driven messaging. Behind-the-scenes content featuring the team humanizes the product and demonstrates that ProLog was built by people who understand the apprenticeship experience. Carousel posts breaking down features make complex functionality scannable.

materials.title: Promotional Videos
materials.description: Video content demonstrates the product workflow and amplifies the emotional benefit of clarity. Feature videos show the dashboard in action, while testimonial-style content creates relatability. Distribution across Instagram, the website, and industry channels reaches both apprentices and institutional stakeholders.

materials.title: Stickers
materials.description: Stickers function as low-friction brand ambassadors distributed at events, job sites, and through training organizations. The visual design mirrors the website's aesthetic while being simple enough to be recognizable at small scale.

developmentConsiderations.title: Development Considerations
developmentConsiderations.description: The design translates into a scalable, accessible product architecture supporting both apprentices and institutional partners.

considerations.title: Component-Based UI Architecture
considerations.description: ProLog's React Native codebase is structured as reusable, isolated components: DashboardCard, ProgressBar, DiscrepancyAlert, QuizQuestion, ResourceLink. This modular approach ensures consistency across features while allowing individual screens to be tested and updated independently. The component library is documented and versioned, enabling rapid feature additions without design system drift.

considerations.title: Accessibility for Neurodivergent Users
considerations.description: Implementation prioritizes WCAG 2.1 AA compliance while exceeding baseline requirements. Specific considerations include: consistent navigation patterns to reduce cognitive load, clear information hierarchy with generous whitespace to minimize visual overwhelm, optional dark mode and custom color contrast settings for sensory preferences, alt text for all visual progress indicators, and reduction of animation to prevent motion sensitivity issues.

considerations.title: Data Architecture for Progress Tracking
considerations.description: The backend maintains separate data streams for user-logged hours, employer-logged hours, and official records, enabling the discrepancy detection engine. A normalized database schema tracks apprentices, trade programs, competency frameworks, hours, and financial records. Data versioning and audit logs ensure accountability while supporting debugging and dispute resolution.

considerations.title: Integration with External Apprenticeship Systems
considerations.description: ProLog integrates with SkilledTradesBC's official records via secure API endpoints, reducing manual data entry burden. The integration supports read operations for pulling official hour totals and competency requirements, while user-logged data can be optionally pushed for employer verification. Fallback modes enable offline functionality on job sites, with sync occurring when connectivity is restored.

marketingPositioning.title: Marketing & Product Positioning
marketingPositioning.description: ProLog's positioning extends beyond interface design to articulate product strategy and market approach.

segments.content: The primary message positions ProLog as a clarity tool rather than another tracking burden. The tagline—'A journey companion for neurodivergent apprentices'—avoids clinical language while directly addressing the target audience. Marketing content emphasizes independence ('No more texts to your supervisor asking how many hours you've logged') and confidence ('Practice with real questions, not generic ones'). Testimonials from apprentices emphasize the emotional benefit of having a single, reliable source of truth.

For training coordinators and apprenticeship programs, ProLog is positioned as a retention tool. The value proposition shifts to operational efficiency: reduced inquiries about progress status, early identification of at-risk apprentices through engagement metrics, and reduced documentation burden through automated hour tracking. Marketing materials highlight the integration with existing systems, reporting capabilities, and support for neurodivergent learners as a competitive differentiator.

ProLog solves three distinct problems: information fragmentation (all progress in one place), cognitive overload (designed specifically for neurodivergent users), and anxiety around accountability (clear tracking with human-readable explanations). The product is not a generic apprenticeship tool; it explicitly recognizes that neurodivergent apprentices face barriers unrelated to capability, and design can remove those barriers.

Initial launch targets a single trade within British Columbia, working directly with established training organizations to recruit cohorts of 50–100 apprentices. Early adopters receive direct support and contribute to a feedback loop that refines features before broader rollout. Success in the initial cohort generates case studies and testimonials for expansion to other trades and provinces. Partnership with neurodivergent advocacy organizations provides credibility and distribution channels.
